Uh they feel like this is their chip to both uh make a lot of money but obviously also to to secure their their position in the future but there's a limit to how much they can push this. We're seeing it in our now casting. So it wasn't really an energy story that surprised the market. And my best guess, you know, we run our now casting across all of the subcategories in in the inflation index is that we get a pretty soft report uh broadly speaking. It's not an energy story. Uh of course, it's partially an energy story, but it's not a standalone story. And my best guess is that we get an a report that prints 0.2 percentage points below the consensus tomorrow. uh which should be a pretty big deal especially given the chart that you just put on the screen here because we had the uh FOMC meeting minutes out uh was it Wednesday or Thursday last week and it was probably the most hawkish message from the um Federal Reserve since 2022 where they had this hiking cycle right so a lot of members are clearly concerned about the inflation outlook uh but it's still an outlook thing if the outlook improves then My best guess is that they'll wait and see. Uh so, you know, I I cannot put enough emphasis on on this inflation report. If it if it prints soft, it should massively uh reprise the front end of the of the curve in the US because everyone is so concerned about uh inflation within that committee. So, they'll probably change their mind if inflation comes down, right? Um it's it's not like they've changed their reaction function. They've they've changed their outlook and that outlook is probably wrong if you ask me. Uh so I see it more as a as a forecasting error than a policy error if that makes sense. >> Yeah.
